name: Wild Rice & Red Onion Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the wild rice in a 1-quart saucepan with water. Stir until the liquid darkens. Drain through a sieve.
Rinse the saucepan and add 1-1/2 cups water and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Bring to boil, then add the rice. Turn heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 to 35 minutes until liquid has evaporated. Check and fluff with fork; set aside covered.
2. Peel and slice onions into 1/8-inch thick pieces. Slice on bias to avoid rings.
3. Heat olive oil in a 2-quart saucepan. Add onions and remaining 1/2 teaspoon salt. Toss to coat. Cover, and cook over low heat for 20 minutes until onions become limp and release liquid.
4. Remove cover, turn heat to medium. Add sugar, butter, rosemary, and black pepper. Mix. Cook uncovered for another 15 minutes or until onions are lightly browned. Begin warming soup plates.
5. Increase heat, add wine. Bring to full simmer, cook until wine almost evaporates. Stir to prevent burning.
6. Add chicken stock, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes. Just before serving, add wild rice and heat briefly.
7. Serve in heated soup plates, sprinkling each with 2 tablespoons Parmesan cheese.
